pi_items.metadata_value: Foreign currency conversion for T2125 income; differentiating CCA classes for tech equipment (Class 50 for PC vs Class 8 for Camera); business-use-of-home expenses
pi_items.verified_value: Foreign currency conversion for T2125 income; differentiating CCA classes for tech equipment (Class 50 for PC vs Class 8 for Camera); business-use-of-home expenses
pi_items.source: Web research confirms CRA classifies electronic data processing equipment (PCs) under Class 50 (55% rate) and other non-specified equipment like cameras under Class 8 (20% rate). The business-use-of-home and foreign conversion are standard T2125 complexities mentioned in the prompt.
Explicitly defined in the task description as the required save path.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a synthetically generated tax scenario for a fictional Ontario taxpayer ('Julian Rossi'). While no setup scripts or data files are physically present in the repository for this specific task, the tax rules referenced in the metadata are authentic. Web research verified that CRA Guide T4002 is the correct guide for Business and Professional Income. Furthermore, CRA's Capital Cost Allowance (CCA) rules confirm that PCs fall under Class 50 (55%) and cameras fall under Class 8 (20%), accurately reflecting the 'key_complexity' claim.
